output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? How to Recover It Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, valid deductions, and how to write a demand letter that gets your money back.
content_outline: Introduction: Why landlords withhold deposits and what the law says
Can a landlord keep your security deposit? (Valid vs. invalid deductions)
How long does a landlord have to return your deposit? (State-by-state deadlines)
What to do when your landlord won't give your deposit back
How to write an effective security deposit dispute letter
When to escalate: Small claims court and penalties
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a strict state-mandated timeline (usually 14–30 days) and cannot deduct for normal wear and tear
A formal security deposit demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover withheld funds without immediate legal fees
Tenants who act quickly and cite specific state laws significantly increase their chances of full deposit recovery or even double damages
output.cta: Don't wait another day. Use DepositBack to generate a state-specific, legally sound security deposit demand letter in minutes—and force your landlord to return what's rightfully yours.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who are problem-aware, have already moved out, and are actively searching for solutions because their landlord is withholding or not returning their security deposit
